name: What to Do with Cherries
recipeInstructions:
1. Pit the cherries using a cherry/olive pitter.
2. Place the pitted cherries in a single layer on a cookie sheet and freeze for 45 minutes to 1 hour, or until individually frozen.
3. Transfer the frozen cherries to Zip Lock Freezer-type bags.
4. Suck the air out of the bags before sealing them to prevent freezer burn.
5. Store the frozen cherries in the freezer for up to 6 months.
6. To use, measure out the desired amount and add to your favorite recipes, such as Cherry Crunch or Cherry Cobbler.
Note: You can also freeze whole cherries without pitting them first. Simply place the cherries on a baking sheet in a single layer and freeze until solid. Then transfer to bags for storage.
